title: R100: documented build progress and milestones
summary: Scottish Government–backed Reaching 100% (R100) roll-out is reported through official channels with premises connected and programme context, including the commercial contract strand and rural emphasis.
caveat: Treat headline figures as government communications; cross-check with evaluation or FOI if you need audit-style rigour.

title: Connecting Scotland: devices, connectivity, and inclusion at scale
summary: Official programme materials describe tens of thousands of households helped with kit and connectivity, with formal evaluation publications on what the programme aimed to measure.
caveat: Self-published evaluation—good for programme aims; triangulate for independent impact claims.

title: Scottish Broadband Voucher Scheme (SBVS)
summary: Official Digital Connectivity pages explain eligibility, subsidy levels, registered suppliers, and how vouchers unlock installs where commercial or R100 build does not reach.
caveat: Rules have changed over time—quote only what the live page says.

title: Ofcom: Scotland connectivity snapshot
summary: Connected Nations reporting includes Scotland-specific findings on full-fibre availability and mobile coverage trends—useful independent regulator evidence for an evidence-friendly tone.
caveat: Coverage metrics are not affordability or lived quality of experience.

title: Social housing full fibre: Hyperoptic Glenavon (case study)
summary: A Scottish Government–published case study describes a Glasgow multi-storey social-rent project combining full fibre, a period of free connectivity, digital skills support, and building-management IoT elements.
primary_label: Scotland’s Full Fibre Charter: Hyperoptic case study
caveat: Industry case study via government charter—an exemplar, not typical everywhere.

title: Mouswald: community voucher pooling and Fibre Community Partnership
summary: Digital Connectivity documents residents combining support (including voucher-style mechanisms) to extend full fibre via Openreach’s Fibre Community Partnership model.
primary_label: Full fibre speeds for Mouswald community
caveat: Project comms can emphasise success; pair with neutral scheme pages (e.g. SBVS) for balance.

title: Library Wi‑Fi expansion (Scottish Library and Information Council)
summary: SLIC documents Scottish Government–linked funding rounds that expanded Wi‑Fi across many public libraries—libraries remain key digital inclusion front doors.
when: Historic funding (2014 on-page); ongoing relevance via library Wi‑Fi
caveat: Partly historical programme documentation—pair with a current council library page for “today” proof if needed.

title: Glasgow’s Digital Housing Strategy 2022–2028
summary: Scotland’s first local authority strategy to address digital housing services and digital exclusion together. The strategy’s own evidence base found that 65% of households in Glasgow’s social rented housing do not use home broadband — with affordability, lack of digital skills, and reluctance to transact online as the primary barriers. Backed by 32 RSLs covering 75% of the sector’s stock. The strategy includes a key safeguard: traditional service methods (face-to-face, telephone) must continue alongside digital, preventing digital-only service design from excluding tenants. Progress is reported through annual housing strategy reviews.
primary_label: Glasgow City Council: Digital Housing Strategy
caveat: The strategy expresses policy intent across four themes (data, digital inclusion, systems, technology). Specific outcome statistics have not yet been published as a standalone evaluation — progress is embedded in annual Local Housing Strategy reviews rather than a dedicated digital strategy audit. Worth following up for published outcomes.
